headline: Rachel was losing money on every kilo she sold to the supermarkets.
subhead: So she helped build a new kind of grocery store. One where the farmer sets the price, and families get food worth eating.
hero_copy:
Rachel Ward's farm in Kempsey, NSW, survived the 2020 bushfires. What nearly finished it wasn't the fire. It was the price the big chains paid for her beef.

Pasture-raised, regenerative, 100% grass-fed. The supermarkets treated it the same as feedlot meat. Rachel knew there had to be a better way.

sections.heading: From paddock to pick-up
sections.body:
Rachel became a co-founder of FarmThru. The idea was simple: connect regenerative farms directly to families, through local collection hubs. No wholesalers. No warehouses. No unnecessary middlemen.

Today, FarmThru works with 50+ farms across NSW. Families order online and collect from their nearest hub. The food is 2-3 days old, not 14-21 days in cold storage like the supermarket supply chain.

sections.heading: A grocery store built around the farm
sections.body:
FarmThru isn't a subscription box or a meal kit. It's an online grocery store where every product traces back to a named farm. Rachel's Farm for regenerative beef. Bundarra Berkshires for heritage pork. Collins Wild Caught for wild-caught salmon. Farmer Brown for eggs.

You know where it came from. You know who grew it. And the farmer earned a fair price for their work.

sections.heading: Now you can be part of it
sections.body:
FarmThru is opening an equity crowdfunding campaign on Birchal in July 2026. You can own a share of the grocery store you buy from. Minimum investment: $50. Cap: $10,000 per investor.

Join the waitlist for first access when the campaign opens.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: This is a pre-registration page for an upcoming equity crowdfunding campaign. No money is being raised at this stage. Any future offer will be made via a disclosure document on Birchal. This is not financial advice. Consider seeking independent financial advice before making any investment decision.

creative_brief: Hero image: Rachel on her farm in Kempsey, natural light, cattle in background. Below-fold: timeline visual (bushfires to regenerative to FarmThru). Product shots from Rachel's Farm. Hub collection lifestyle image. Warm, documentary-style photography throughout.
